name: Meta DE Study Guide
description:
Technical Interviews
What can you expect?
These three technical interviews collectively will assess your skills for product sense, data modeling, SQL, ETL, and coding in Python. You'll be using our virtual whiteboard interfaces Coderpad and Excalidraw (optional) to communicate ideas, write actual SQL and Python, draw schemas, and visualize data and process flow.

One of the three technical interviews will be AI-assisted. This means you may use AI tools during most sections of that interview — but we are evaluating your judgment, reasoning, and technical decision-making, so AI output on its own is not treated as an answer. Here's what to keep in mind:
The CoderPad session used in the interview will include a built-in AI assistant — this is the only AI tool you are permitted to use. You may not use external AI tools such as ChatGPT, Gemini, Copilot, or any other LLM during the interview.
Your final answer is what you write on the left side of CoderPad. If you include AI-generated text there, we may ask you to explain it and why you chose it.
Please explain your approach in your own words. Your interviewer will ask follow-ups to confirm your understanding of anything AI helped generate.
How to use AI well: Use it to brainstorm options, draft an approach, or suggest checks — then filter, prioritize, validate, and decide.

How to use AI well: Use it to brainstorm options, draft an approach, or suggest checks — then filter, prioritize, validate, and decide.


What do we look for?
Your interviewer will be thinking about how your skills and experience might help Meta as well as how you tackle problems you’re not as familiar with. In your Technical interview, your interviewer will assess your performance on three focus areas:
Product Sense
Focus Area 2: Data Modeling
Focus Area 3: ETL (SQL & Python)

One of the three technical interviews will be AI-assisted. In that interview, your interviewer will also assess:
AI Usage: How effectively do you use AI tools and prompts to aid in answering questions across focus areas? How do you evaluate AI-generated output and incorporate it into your code or responses? We are looking for your ability to leverage AI as a tool while demonstrating independent judgment and technical ownership of the final solution.
The remaining two technical interviews will not involve AI tools — you will be expected to demonstrate your skills independently without AI assistance.



How to prep
Product Sense
At Meta, we expect data engineers to have not only a strong technical aptitude but also a keen product sense. All three technical interviews will be case studies of typical product challenges that we solve with data. Your interviewer will assess your ability to think critically about the needs of the product in each scenario and how you translate those needs into a robust technical solution. One way you can practice this is to think about metrics that companies similar to Meta might use.

For example, you can look at their financial statements to see what metrics they deem strategically important. From there, you should think through how you would calculate those metrics, and what you would do if they started moving unexpectedly.
Met Quarterly Results
How Would You Measure the Success or Failure of a Product Feature? ​
Product Sense Problems for Data Scientists
Data Visualization

Data Modeling
You’ll brainstorm the data needs of a user product. Then you’ll design a data mart to support analytics use cases and write select SQL statements to produce specific results. To practice data modeling, go through the main products of several large tech companies, such as Meta, LinkedIn, or Amazon. Ask yourself, how would you model each function you use from the organization’s product? Create logging designs for how you think data should be captured, then design data models to support analytical queries and reporting needs for those products. Here are some resources which might help you with prep work for data modeling:
Data Modeling Tutorial
Data Modeling in Big Data
Data Model
Data Mart
Dimensional Modeling
Denormalization
Dimensional Modeling Techniques

SQL
We’ll ask you to focus on basic SQL constructs. Practice all types of joins, aggregate functions, analytical functions, set operators, and subqueries. You can take the work you did in data modeling, and practice how you’d load and transform the data from the logging sources into your target tables using SQL. During this exercise, also think through how you might help improve efficiency and scalability for processing large data volumes. Use the links below to practice:
SQL Tutorial
PostgreSQL Exercises ​
Netflix Case Study
Data engineering at Meta: High-Level Overview of the internal tech stack
Advanced SQL Interview Questions and Answers

Coding
Make sure that you know all the data structures and how to manipulate them well. Familiarize yourself with string, sets operations, etc., in your programming in Python, and make sure you understand how dictionaries, lists, and loops work.
Python Guide
HackerRank
CodingBat (Python)
Leetcode
Python for Everyone Course
Python Data Structures Course

AI Usage
As noted above, one of your three technical interviews will be AI-assisted. Here are some tips to help you make the most of it:
Understand the Output: Whether AI generates code, SQL queries, data model designs, or metric recommendations, it is crucial that you understand what was produced and why. Be prepared to explain the logic, identify trade-offs, and describe how you would modify or extend the output.
Communicate Your Thought Process: Just like in traditional interviews, vocalize your thought process as you work through the problem. Walk your interviewer through how you are using AI and why, not just the output it produces.
Managing AI Hallucinations: Be aware that AI can hallucinate or lead you in an unhelpful direction — for example, suggesting metrics that don't align with the product scenario, producing incorrect joins, or generating flawed schema designs. Validate AI-generated output before incorporating it into your solution. Your interviewer will also help ensure that the AI doesn't drive the conversation too far off course.
Unblocking Yourself: Leverage AI to unblock yourself if you get stuck — whether it's a syntax issue in SQL or Python, brainstorming dimensions for a data model, or exploring product metrics for a scenario you're less familiar with. This is one of the most effective ways to use AI during the interview — treat it as a productivity tool, not a substitute for your own reasoning.
Practice Set: It is highly recommended to access the CoderPad practice environment prior to your interview to familiarize yourself with the new features. You can view the CoderPad UI via the Preparation Hub in Career Profile.
